sections.name: Dominica
lines: v. Now to the king of ages, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

sections.name: Feria
lines: !Zach 8:19
v. Love ye truth and peace, said the Almighty Lord.

sections.name: Per Annum
lines: !2 Thess 3:5
v. And the Lord direct your hearts, in the charity of God, and the patience of Christ.

sections.name: Adv
lines: v. O Lord, have mercy on us: for we have waited for thee: be thou our arm in the morning, and our salvation in the time of trouble.

sections.name: Nat
lines: v. They shall perish, but thou shalt continue: and they shall all grow old as a garment. And as a vesture shalt thou change them, and they shall be changed: but thou art the selfsame, and thy years shall not fail.

sections.name: Epi
lines: v. All they from Saba shall come, bringing gold and frankincense: and shewing forth praise to the Lord.

sections.name: Asc
lines: !Acts 1:11
v. Ye men of Galilee, why stand you looking up to heaven? This Jesus who is taken up from you into heaven, shall so come, as you have seen him going into heaven.

sections.name: Quad
lines: v. Seek ye the Lord, while he may be found: call upon him, while he is near.

sections.name: Quad5
lines: v. I have not turned away my face from them that rebuked me, and spit upon me. The Lord God is my helper, therefore am I not confounded.

sections.name: Pasch
lines: v. If you be risen with Christ, seek the things that are above; where Christ is sitting at the right hand of God: Mind the things that are above, not the things that are upon the earth.

sections.name: Pent
lines: !Acts 2:11
v. Jews also, and Proselytes, Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our tongues the wonderful works of God.

sections.name: Responsory
lines: R.br. Christ, Son of the living God, * have mercy on us.
R. Christ, Son of the living God, * have mercy on us.
V. Thou, who sittest at the right hand of the Father.
R. Have mercy on us.
&Gloria1
R. Christ, Son of the living God, * have mercy on us.

sections.name: Versum
lines: V. Arise, O Christ, and help us.
R. And deliver us for thy name's sake.

sections.name: Responsory Adv
lines: Thou that shouldst come into the world.

sections.name: Responsory Nat
lines: Thou that wast born of the Virgin Mary.

sections.name: Responsory Epi
lines: Thou that appeared today.

sections.name: Responsory Pasch
lines: Thou that arose from the dead.

sections.name: Responsory Asc
lines: Thou that hast gone up above the stars.

sections.name: Responsory Pent
lines: Thou, who sittest at the right hand of the Father.

sections.name: Responsory Corp
lines: Thou that wast born of the Virgin Mary.

sections.name: Responsory Heart
lines: Such grace, O Jesu, from thy Heart.

sections.name: Hymnus Prima
lines: v. Now in the sun's new dawning ray,
Lowly of heart, our God we pray
That he from harm may keep us free
In all the deeds this day shall see.
May fear of him our tongues restrain,
Lest strife unguarded speech should stain:
His favouring care our guardian be,
Lest our eyes feed on vanity.
May every heart be pure from sin
And folly find no place therein:
Scant need of food, excess denied,
Wear down in us the body's pride.
That when the light of day is gone,
And night in course shall follow on.
We, free from cares the world affords,
May chant the praise that is our Lord's.
* All laud to God the Father be,
All praise, Eternal Son, to thee:
All glory, as is ever meet,
To God the holy Paraclete.
Amen.
